name: Silk Road Caravan (ca. 1300)
description: A stylized caravan route from Xi'an to Constantinople, representing centuries of overland trade.

moments.title: Eastern Terminus
moments.text: Caravans assemble near Chang'an (Xi'an), loaded with silk, tea, and porcelain.

moments.title: Kashgar Crossroads
moments.text: Merchants and cultures mix at the edge of the Tarim Basin, where routes diverge around the mountains.

moments.title: Desert Oases
moments.text: Oases in Khurasan and Persia sustain caravans with water, fodder, and new wares.

moments.title: Western Terminus
moments.text: Caravans reach Constantinople, where goods spread into Europe via Mediterranean trade.

points.label: Dunhuang – Gateway to the western deserts
Near the Caspian – western steppe route
